CRITICAL.title: S3 Bucket Public via ACL
CRITICAL.description: Bucket 'my-public-bucket' has public read access via ACL.
CRITICAL.recommendation: Remove public access from bucket ACL or enable S3 Block Public Access.

CRITICAL.title: Root Account MFA Not Enabled
CRITICAL.description: The AWS root account does not have MFA enabled.
CRITICAL.recommendation: Enable MFA on the root account immediately.

HIGH.title: SSH Root Login Enabled
HIGH.description: Root login via SSH is set to 'yes'. This allows direct root access and should be disabled.
HIGH.recommendation: Set 'PermitRootLogin no' in sshd_config to prevent direct root login.

HIGH.title: SSH Password Authentication Enabled
HIGH.description: Password authentication is enabled, making the system vulnerable to brute force attacks.
HIGH.recommendation: Set 'PasswordAuthentication no' and use SSH key pairs for authentication.

HIGH.title: SSH Open to the World
HIGH.description: 2 security groups allow SSH (22) from anywhere.
HIGH.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to known IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager.

MEDIUM.title: S3 Buckets Without Default Encryption
MEDIUM.description: 3 buckets do not have default encryption enabled.
MEDIUM.recommendation: Enable default encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS) for all buckets.

MEDIUM.title: S3 Buckets Without Versioning
MEDIUM.description: 5 buckets do not have versioning enabled.
MEDIUM.recommendation: Enable versioning for data protection and recovery.

MEDIUM.title: Weak Password Policy
MEDIUM.description: Password policy has issues: Missing symbol requirement; Min length 8, should be 14+
MEDIUM.recommendation: Strengthen password policy with complexity and expiry requirements.
